message.content: {"rulings": [{"id": "531c4505-38ed-4d66-9fc8-d76575e7a367", "relation": "supersedes", "reason": "The dev server moved to port 4000 by 1 Jul 2026; port 3000 is no longer in use."}]}
message.reasoning_content:
Let me analyze this case:

NEW CLAIM:
- observed: 2026-07-01
- claim: "The dev server is running on port 4000"

OLD CLAIM:
- id: 531c4505-38ed-4d66-9fc8-d76575e7a367
- observed: 2026-04-06
- claim: "The development server for the Meridian project runs on port 3000."

Analysis:
- The new claim says the dev server is on port 4000 (observed 2026-07-01)
- The old claim says the dev server is on port 3000 (observed 2026-04-06)
- These are about the same thing (dev server port)
- They CANNOT both be true at the same time - a server can't be running on both port 3000 and port 4000 simultaneously
- The new claim is MORE RECENT (July 2026 vs April 2026)
- This is a clear case of supersedes - the port changed from 3000 to 4000

The new claim makes the old one false. The dev server moved from port 3000 to port 4000. This is a supersession.
